title: Baked Crispy Fish with Minted Peas & Yogurt Tartar
description: A lightened-up take on crispy fish — baked instead of fried, with a yogurt-based tartar sauce and buttery minted peas served over grains.

ingredients.name: white fish fillets (sole, cod, or halibut)

instructions.text: Rinse quinoa or farro and cook according to package directions. Set aside covered to keep warm.
Make the tartar sauce: mix yogurt, cornichons, capers, shallot, dill, and lemon zest and juice in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Refrigerate until serving.
Preheat oven to 425°F with a wire rack set inside a baking sheet.
Bread the fish: season fillets, coat each in flour (shake off excess), dip in whisked egg, then press into panko until well coated. Place on the wire rack and brush or drizzle with avocado oil.
Bake fish until golden and cooked through, about 12–15 minutes, flipping halfway if needed for even browning.

Make the minted peas: boil salted water and cook frozen peas about 4 minutes until tender. Drain well, return to pot with butter, and crush lightly with a potato masher. Off heat, stir in lemon zest and mint. Season with salt and pepper. Hold the lemon juice until just before serving.
Stir lemon juice into peas just before serving. Plate the grain, top with minted peas, the baked fish, and a generous spoonful of tartar sauce.

notes:
Skip the breading and pan-sear in avocado oil for a leaner, gluten-free version.

Sole, cod, and halibut all work well here — sole is most delicate, halibut is meatiest.

The tartar sauce keeps well refrigerated for 3–4 days and doubles as a dip for vegetables.
